stories.headline: Iran rejects second round of talks with US Two sources told The Jerusalem Post that Washington aims to first reach a framework agreement before negotiating the details.
stories.summary: Iran has rejected a second round of talks with the US, casting doubt on efforts to revive diplomacy. The US had aimed to reach a framework agreement before negotiating details on Iran's nuclear program. Recent high-level contacts between the two countries in Pakistan had sought to ease tensions, but Iran has now rejected US calls to halt uranium enrichment. However, Tehran has expressed willingness to discuss the "level and purity" of enrichment. The rejection comes after a fragile two-week ceasefire was agreed upon last week, following nearly six weeks of fighting. The development is a setback to diplomatic efforts to resolve the nuclear issue, with Washington seeking a framework agreement before proceeding with detailed negotiations. The move raises concerns about the future of US-Iran relations and the potential for renewed tensions.

stories.headline: Strait of Hormuz: No more Mr nice guy - Trump renews threats on Iran United States President Donald Trump has again renewed his threat to destroy Iran's infrastructure if no deal is reached.
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump has renewed his threat to destroy Iran's infrastructure if a deal is not reached. He claimed Iran fired bullets at a French ship and a UK freighter in the Strait of Hormuz, violating a two-week ceasefire. Trump stated that without a deal, the US will "knock out every single power plant, and every single bridge, in Iran." This comes after he previously pulled back on similar threats, subject to Iran agreeing to a ceasefire and reopening the Strait. Vice President JD Vance is set to lead a delegation to Pakistan, including envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner, amid ongoing tensions between the US, Iran, and Israel. Trump's latest statement marks a return to a harder stance, with him declaring "no more Mr nice guy."

stories.headline: DR Congo, M23 armed group agree to ease aid, free prisoners International, MNA | The DR Congo government and the M23 group agreed to ease humanitarian aid deliveries and release prisoners within 10 days, according to a j
stories.summary: The DR Congo government and the M23 armed group have agreed to ease humanitarian aid deliveries and release prisoners within 10 days. The agreement was reached following talks in Switzerland, as stated in a joint statement on Sunday. The parties aim to build confidence through these efforts. A memorandum of understanding for a ceasefire monitoring mechanism was also signed, which will begin conducting surveillance. The M23 group was formed by deserters of the National Congress for the Defence of the People, who had integrated into the Armed Forces of the Democratic Republic of the Congo after a 2009 peace agreement. The agreement marks a step towards resolving the conflict and addressing humanitarian needs in the region. The release of prisoners and easing of aid deliveries are expected to take place within the specified 10-day timeframe.

stories.headline: Hero principal who tackled school shooter is crowned prom king Suspect wanted to carry out a mass shooting ‘like the Columbine shooters did,’ he told investigators
stories.summary: Victor Lee Hawkins, 20, a former student, was arrested and charged with unlawfully carrying a firearm, shooting with intent to kill, and pointing a firearm after a shooting at an Oklahoma high school. Hawkins allegedly told investigators he wanted to carry out a mass shooting like the Columbine attackers, who killed 13 students and a teacher over two decades ago. He took his father's firearms without permission and went to the school with the intention of carrying out the shooting. The school's principal, who was shot in the leg, tackled Hawkins, preventing further harm. The principal was recently crowned prom king in recognition of his heroic actions. Hawkins' motives and further details are still under investigation, with authorities having obtained an affidavit detailing his alleged intentions.

stories.headline: New Mac Studio and Touchscreen MacBook Pro Could Be Delayed by Memory Shortage
stories.summary: Apple's upcoming Mac Studio and MacBook Pro launches may be delayed due to a global memory shortage. The M5 Mac Studio is now expected to arrive in October at the earliest, while the touchscreen MacBook Pro is likely to be released in early 2027. According to Bloomberg's Mark Gurman, the delays are caused by an industry-wide memory chip shortage. This shortage is affecting the production of the 14-inch and 16-inch MacBook Pro models, as well as the new Mac Studio. As a result, the release dates for these devices have been pushed back, with customers potentially having to wait longer to upgrade to the latest models. The memory shortage is a global issue, impacting the tech industry as a whole, and Apple is not the only company affected by the supply constraints.

stories.headline: Windows 11 to get a major reliability update in May with faster clipboard, stable taskbar, storage and more
stories.summary: Microsoft is preparing a major Windows 11 reliability update for May 2026, bringing improvements to File Explorer, taskbar, Settings, clipboard history, and input systems. The update aims to make the operating system more responsive, reliable, and consistent, with reduced system resource usage. Windows 11 builds 26200.8313 and 26100.8313 have been released in the Release Preview Channel, featuring changes that will be included in the May update. The rollout is scheduled to start on May 12, 2026, at 1PM Eastern Time. The update will be deployed in two phases: gradual rollout and normal rollout. Microsoft expects Windows 11 to feel faster and more consistent by the end of the year, with significant technical and interface improvements. The company has outlined various enhancements, including a faster clipboard and stable taskbar, to enhance the overall user experience.

stories.headline: Nikon Teases the Impending Arrival of Autofocus Z Cinema Lenses
stories.summary: Nikon has released a video teaser for its upcoming autofocus Z cinema lenses, following previous announcements about its entry into dedicated cinema optics. The company has not disclosed extensive details, but the teaser hints at the soon-to-arrive lenses offering advanced features. Nikon had earlier confirmed the development of Z Cinema lenses, with a speculated roadmap including primes and zooms over the next two years. The Z CINEMA series aims to meet a wide range of needs in film production, with Nikon and RED collaborating to offer an extensive lineup of cinema-oriented products. A dedicated Instagram account for Z Cinema has been launched, which will serve as a hub for updates on lenses, events, and ecosystem news. Details remain scarce, but the teaser suggests that Nikon is making progress in its cinema lens development.

stories.headline: ‘Dangerous’ AI training plans could be a runner’s Achilles heel
stories.summary: Runners using AI-generated training plans from apps like Runna may be at increased risk of injury due to a lack of personalized feedback and biomechanical analysis. Experts warn that these plans, based on averages, cannot assess individual movement or lifestyle factors, potentially leading to overtraining or incorrect technique. While AI algorithms can tailor plans to a runner's progress and performance, they may not account for unique factors that can contribute to injury. Personal trainers and nutritionists have expressed concern, stating that AI plans are incomplete and may not provide the necessary guidance for safe and effective training. With 2 million monthly users, Runna's AI-powered training plans have raised concerns among experts, highlighting the need for caution when using AI-generated fitness plans, especially in high-impact sports like long-distance running. Injury risk can never be entirely eliminated, but informed training can help mitigate it.

stories.headline: Bavarian Football Works FC Bayern has won another Bundesliga title!
stories.summary: Bayern Munich has won its 35th German football title, securing its 13th Bundesliga title in 14 years. The team clinched the title with a 4-2 victory over VfB Stuttgart, using a mixed lineup to put together a complete effort. This marks the club's second consecutive title, following Bayer Leverkusen's win in the 2023/24 season. The win solidifies Bayern Munich's dominance in German football, with the team having won 13 of the past 14 Bundesliga titles. The title is also the first major trophy of Harry Kane's career. Bayern Munich's record-breaking season could still get better, with the team having wrapped up the title with a strong performance against Stuttgart. The victory illustrates the club's modern and historical dominance of German football.

stories.headline: Nikola Jokic Catches Accusation From Timberwolves Coach Minnesota Timberwolves head coach Chris Finch accused Nikola Jokic of being a foul-baiter after the Game 1 matchup.
stories.summary: Minnesota Timberwolves head coach Chris Finch accused Nikola Jokic of being a foul-baiter after the Game 1 matchup. Finch was later fined $35,000 by the NBA for directing inappropriate language toward game officials and failing to leave the court promptly after being ejected. The incident occurred during a game against the Thunder, where Finch was assessed two technical fouls and ejected less than midway through the first quarter. The fine was announced by James Jones, Executive Vice President, Head of Basketball Operations. Finch's ejection and subsequent fine may have implications for his coaching style and interactions with referees. The accusation against Jokic and Finch's behavior have drawn attention to the coach's approach to game officiating. The NBA's fine reflects its efforts to maintain professional conduct among coaches and players.

stories.headline: Juventus best positioned to sign veteran Barcelona ace likely to leave the club - report Earlier in the day, it was reported that Robert Lewandowski’s agent, Pini Zahavi, is set to meet the representatives of Juventus to discuss a potential move to
stories.summary: Robert Lewandowski's agent, Pini Zahavi, is set to meet with Juventus representatives to discuss a potential move to Turin. The striker is considering his future at Barcelona, and Juventus is one of the clubs showing interest in signing him. This development comes ahead of the summer transfer window. Lewandowski's potential move to Juventus would be a significant addition to the team. Juventus has a history of signing high-profile players, such as Cristiano Ronaldo in 2018, which elevated the club's popularity. The meeting between Zahavi and Juventus representatives is expected to explore the possibility of Lewandowski joining the Italian club. With Juventus expressing interest, they appear to be well-positioned to sign the veteran Barcelona ace, who is likely to leave the Spanish club. The potential transfer is gaining attention, with Juventus being a strong contender.

stories.headline: 'That's where Cristiano Ronaldo played!' - Endrick reveals 'affection' for Man Utd as Real Madrid loanee drops Premier League transfer hint Endrick has opened up on his deep-rooted admiration for Manchester United, admitting that the Premier League giants hold a special place in his heart.
stories.summary: Endrick, a 16-year-old Brazilian forward on loan at Lyon from Real Madrid, has expressed his admiration for Manchester United. He revealed that his affection for the Premier League club stems from Cristiano Ronaldo's successful stint there. Endrick also praised the Premier League, citing its exciting matches and high-quality clubs. His admiration for Manchester United is notable, given his current affiliation with Real Madrid. Endrick had previously mentioned that Cristiano Ronaldo and Vinicius Junior influenced his decision to join Real Madrid from Palmeiras in July 2024. The young forward's comments have sparked speculation about a potential future transfer to the Premier League, with Manchester United being a possible destination. Endrick's admiration for the club and the league could be an indication of his future aspirations.

stories.headline: South Korean President Lee Jae-myung arrives for three-day state visit; 'Special Strategic Partnership' in focus
stories.summary: South Korean President Lee Jae-myung has arrived in New Delhi for a three-day state visit, the first by a South Korean leader in over eight years. He was received by Union Minister of State for Corporate Affairs Harsh Malhotra at the airport. President Lee's visit aims to strengthen the India-South Korea Special Strategic Partnership, with a focus on enhancing cooperation in key areas such as trade, artificial intelligence, semiconductors, and emerging technologies. During his visit, he will hold key meetings with Prime Minister Narendra Modi and President Droupadi Murmu to discuss these issues. The visit also seeks to reboot cooperation in defence production and green energy, among other sectors. President Lee is accompanied by First Lady Kim Hea Kyung. His itinerary includes discussions on bilateral relations and mutual interests.

stories.headline: Diplomats’ families and students fleeing Iran return to India
stories.summary: Over 200 Indian citizens, including diplomats' families and students, have been evacuated from Iran amid ongoing conflict. A second batch of students stranded in the violence-hit country has returned to India, with many hailing from Jammu and Kashmir. The evacuations were carried out under Operation Sindhu, launched by India to rescue its nationals from Iran. The students, who were relocated to Armenia via the land border, shared accounts of the escalating conflict and expressed gratitude to the Indian government for its swift response. The first batch of nearly 70 Indians and a subsequent batch of 110 students have returned home, marking a tense chapter for hundreds of families across India. The evacuations were prioritized by the embassies, ensuring the safe return of Indian nationals from Iran, where protests have left over 3,000 dead.

stories.headline: CBI arrests DGCA Deputy Director General on graft charge
stories.summary: The Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) arrested the Deputy Director General at the Airworthiness Directorate of the Directorate General of Civil Aviation (DGCA) and a representative of a private company in a bribery case. Mudavath Devula, Deputy Director General, was allegedly seeking ₹2.5 lakh to grant a licence to a drone manufacturing unit. The CBI seized ₹37 lakh in cash and valuables during searches in Delhi. The arrest was made on April 19, with the CBI also detaining corporate Senior Vice President Bharat Mathur. The bribery amount of ₹2.5 lakh was recovered, and the CBI is investigating the matter further. The arrest is related to the granting of a licence to a drone manufacturing unit, highlighting corruption in Indian aviation regulation. The CBI's action is a significant development in the case, with further investigations underway.

stories.headline: West Bengal BJP leader Dinesh Trivedi expected to be new Indian envoy to Bangladesh
stories.summary: Dinesh Trivedi, a former Union minister and senior BJP leader from West Bengal, is expected to be appointed as India's next High Commissioner to Bangladesh. The move is seen as an effort to reset ties between the two neighboring countries. Trivedi's appointment is crucial as Bangladesh is facing an energy crisis, and India is likely to play a key role in addressing it. As a political heavyweight, Trivedi's experience and connections are expected to help strengthen bilateral relations. The appointment is yet to be officially announced, but sources have confirmed that Trivedi is the chosen candidate for the position. His posting as High Commissioner will be significant in enhancing cooperation between India and Bangladesh on various fronts, including trade, energy, and security.

stories.headline: TMC ‘Betrayed’ Women, Will Be Punished In Assembly Polls: PM Modi
stories.summary: PM Modi accused the Trinamool Congress (TMC) of betraying women and opposing women's empowerment in West Bengal. He claimed the TMC blocked the women's reservation bill and conspired with Congress to prevent women from becoming MPs and MLAs in large numbers. Modi stated that the TMC opposes women's empowerment because women are challenging their 'mahajungleraj' in the state. He asserted that women voters in West Bengal would punish the TMC in the upcoming assembly polls for their actions. Modi said, "The TMC has once again betrayed the sisters of West Bengal. The women of Bengal will punish TMC in this election." He emphasized that the TMC's actions are a result of women challenging their rule in the state, and that they will face consequences in the upcoming polls.

stories.headline: Bengal's women will punish TMC over quota betrayal, says Modi
stories.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi accused the Trinamool Congress (TMC) of betraying women by blocking the Constitution (131st Amendment) Bill, 2026, which aimed to reserve 33% of seats in Parliament and state assemblies for women. He claimed that women voters in West Bengal would punish the TMC in the upcoming assembly polls for opposing women's empowerment. Modi alleged that the TMC and Congress obstructed the passage of the bill, and pitched the BJP as a pro-empowerment and security party. He also promised that if the BJP comes to power in West Bengal, it would provide up to Rs 1.5 lakh to women under the Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana to build homes. The comments were made during an election rally in Bankura district, where Modi addressed the crowd and criticized the TMC's stance on women's reservation.

stories.headline: West Bengal elections: TMC 'betrayed' women, will be punished in assembly polls, says PM Modi
stories.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi accused the Trinamool Congress (TMC) of betraying women by blocking the Constitution (131st Amendment) Bill, 2026, which aimed for 33% reservation in Parliament and state assemblies. He claimed the TMC conspired with Congress to oppose the bill, thereby opposing women's empowerment. Modi stated that women in West Bengal would punish the TMC in the upcoming assembly polls for their actions. The Prime Minister made these comments on Sunday, asserting that the TMC's decision would have consequences in the state's elections. He emphasized that women voters in West Bengal would hold the TMC accountable for their stance against the bill, which aimed to increase women's representation in legislative bodies. The assembly polls are scheduled to take place in the state, with the TMC's actions on women's empowerment likely to be a key issue.

stories.headline: No fourth headline available, only three provided
stories.summary: A global news update is available, covering various topics including international and world news, breaking stories, and current events. The latest headlines, news, and videos can be found on APNews.com, a source for independent journalism from over 150 countries. The New York Times also provides live news, investigations, opinion, photos, and videos from around the world. Current news coverage includes U.S. and world news, entertainment, health, business, technology, politics, and sports. Google News aggregates comprehensive and up-to-date news from sources worldwide. The latest news can be accessed through these online platforms, providing a wide range of information on global events and topics. Users can follow the latest developments and stay informed about what's happening around the world.

stories.headline: Stock Markets Today: Sensex, Nifty50 Turn Volatile on West Asia Crisis
stories.summary: The Indian stock markets, Sensex and Nifty50, have turned volatile due to the ongoing crisis in West Asia. The markets had previously risen, with Sensex jumping 500 points and Nifty settling above 17,350, driven by factors such as easing crude oil prices. However, the West Asia crisis has ignited concerns over a potential ceasefire breach, causing market volatility. According to VK Vijayakumar, Chief Investment Strategist at Geojit Investments Limited, the market will remain volatile in the near-term due to the deescalation-escalation drama in the West Asian conflict. Crude oil prices had slipped below $100-a-barrel, providing some relief, but the Strait of Hormuz crisis remains unresolved, contributing to elevated volatility. The Indian market is expected to remain resilient for now, but experts warn of potential second-order inflation pain. The market's volatility is likely to continue until the West Asia crisis is resolved.

stories.headline: Geopolitical tensions, corporate results to steer markets
stories.summary: Indian stock markets will be influenced by the US-Iran conflict, crude oil price fluctuations, and quarterly earnings reports from major corporates. Geopolitical tensions may outweigh solid earnings in the near term. The US results season is gaining pace, with Goldman Sachs reporting better-than-expected results due to a recovery in underwriting and dealmaking. Major US banks are expected to post over $40 billion in trading revenue for the first quarter, driven by sharp market movements. Companies like Dabur have seen domestic performance offset challenges in international markets, particularly in West Asia, where tensions have caused demand disruptions and supply chain constraints. Geopolitical tensions affect markets through supply chain disruption, impacting global companies that rely on international manufacturing and logistics, leading to delays and higher costs. Foreign investor activity will also play a crucial role in shaping market trends.

stories.headline: Indian stock market: How are Sensex and Nifty likely to perform next week amid US-Iran war?
stories.summary: Indian stock market performance is expected to be influenced by the US-Iran war situation. The market is likely to open higher amid hopes of renewed peace talks between the US and Iran. The Nifty index has risen over 2,200 points from recent lows, but this recovery will be tested due to war-related uncertainties and key banking results. Analysts predict a market upside of 10-15% if the US-Iran war de-escalates, as easing tensions would boost investor sentiment. The GIFT Nifty indicates a gap-up open, trading at 24,202 with a premium. Corporate earnings, oil prices, and the rupee's movement will also steer the market's direction. Key banking results, including HDFC Bank, ICICI Bank, and Yes Bank, will be closely watched. The market's performance will depend on how it balances geopolitical tensions with domestic macroeconomic factors.

stories.headline: Ahead of Market: 10 things that will decide stock market action on Monday
stories.summary: Indian markets experienced a volatile week, with the Nifty falling 2.09% to 22,819.60 and the Sensex down 2.25% due to Iran-US tensions and elevated energy prices. However, the market later rebounded, with the Nifty closing above 18,200, led by gains in banking, auto, and consumer stocks. The market sentiments turned bearish on Friday, with 3,001 stocks declining and 955 advancing on the BSE. The Nifty wiped out most of its Friday gains amid F&O expiry, with the Sensex dropping over 700 points. Out of 4,059 stocks traded, 103 remained unchanged. The market's performance was influenced by global trends and domestic factors, setting the stage for the next trading session. Investors will be watching key factors, including global market trends and economic indicators, to decide the stock market action.

stories.headline: [The Times] University system is broken, says student regulator’s ex-director John Blake says greater competition has failed to improve the standard of teaching
stories.summary: John Blake, the outgoing Director for Fair Access and Participation at the Office for Students, has stated that the university system is broken. He believes that increased competition has not led to improvements in teaching standards. Blake's four-year term ends this year, and he has decided not to stay for another term. During his tenure, he led a revamp of universities and worked on implementing changes, but noted that attempts at change are often frustrated in the final stages. He cited the example of institutions spending time working out adjustments for students during the Covid period, only to see them not implemented. Blake also thinks the current student loans system is "doomed" and could be replaced by a tax to protect taxpayers. An interim director will be appointed while a recruitment process takes place to fill Blake's role.

stories.headline: [The Times] Why frustrated Uber drivers are launching a rival app Ride Nuff promises a fairer deal for London’s taxi drivers and customers by taking a smaller commission than Uber — and it has just gained a licence to operate
stories.summary: Frustrated Uber drivers in London are launching a rival app called Ride Nuff, promising a fairer deal for both drivers and customers. Ride Nuff has gained a licence to operate and will take a smaller commission than Uber. This move comes after Uber faced criticism for its treatment of drivers and its business practices. Recently, it was revealed that Uber's new contract allows the company to take up to 49% commission on fares. An academic paper also found that many Uber drivers are earning substantially less since the introduction of a dynamic pricing algorithm in 2023. Additionally, Uber has faced regulatory issues, including the non-renewal of its private hire operator licence in London in 2017 due to concerns over its use of a tool called Greyball to skirt local regulations. Ride Nuff aims to provide a more equitable alternative.

stories.headline: [The Motley Fool] Here's the 1 thing to Watch When Lemonade Releases Earnings on April 29 The stock dropped after the last earnings report
stories.summary: Lemonade is set to release its fiscal 2026 Q1 earnings on April 29. The company's stock dropped after its last earnings report, but the upcoming release provides an opportunity for redemption. Investors should focus on key metrics such as revenue, earnings, and balance-sheet strength. The insurance company's long-term demand and financial stability will also be under scrutiny. The Motley Fool has highlighted the importance of this earnings report, citing the potential for the company to rebound from its previous stock drop. With the release scheduled for later this month, investors will be closely watching to see if Lemonade can meet or exceed expectations and regain investor confidence. The report will provide insight into the company's financial health and future prospects.

stories.headline: [PR Newswire] related cognitive symptoms after starting VYEPTI® (eptinezumab) /PRNewswire/ -- H. Lundbeck A/S (Lundbeck) today presented new real-world 6-month results from the INFUSE study at the American Academy of Neurology (AAN) 2026
stories.summary: H. Lundbeck A/S presented new real-world 6-month results from the INFUSE study at the American Academy of Neurology 2026. The study evaluated the effectiveness of VYEPTI (eptinezumab) in adults with migraine who had failed at least one preventive anti-calcitonin gene-related peptide treatment. The findings showed meaningful improvements in patient-reported global status and headache burden after switching to intravenous eptinezumab. The INFUSE study observed patient-reported migraine-related cognitive symptoms at baseline and changes over 6 months. The results also indicated longer interictal periods in a post-hoc analysis of the DELIVER trial. The data suggests that VYEPTI can provide real-world improvements for adults with migraine who have previously failed other preventive therapies. The study's results were also presented at the 2026 Headache and the American Headache Society 67th Annual Scientific Meeting.
